title: YenGo SGF Custom Properties Schema
description: Schema defining YenGo's custom SGF properties for tsumego puzzles. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for SGF property validation.

version_history.changes: Added ac (analysis completeness) field to YQ quality metrics. YQ format now q:{1-5};rc:{N};hc:{0-2};ac:{0-3}. hc expanded to 3-level (0=none, 1=correctness markers, 2=teaching text). ac tracks pipeline processing state (0=untouched, 1=enriched, 2=ai_solved, 3=verified). All four fields are now required.

Extended YL to support optional chapter/position suffix (slug:CHAPTER/POSITION format). Chapter is a string identifier, position is an integer. Enables source-provided learning sequence preservation. Backward compatible: bare slugs still valid.

Consolidated YS (source) and YI (run_id) into YM pipeline metadata JSON. YS and YI no longer emitted as separate properties. YM format: {t, f, s, i}. Backward compat: parser still reads legacy YS/YI if present.

Added YM (pipeline metadata JSON) embedding trace_id and original_filename directly in SGF. Eliminates trace map sidecar files. Schema bump v10→v12 (skip v11) to mark rebuild-centric architecture change.
Added YL (collection membership) for curated puzzle collections (spec-126). Comma-separated sorted slugs referencing config/collections.json.
Added YS (source adapter ID) for accurate publish log tracking when stages run separately.
SGF enrichment refactor (spec-053): Removed YH1/YH2/YH3 (no backward compat), standardized GN format, SO must be empty, remove root C[], keep move C[], no empty lines in output.
Compact hints format - YH[hint1|hint2|...] replaces YH1/YH2/YH3 (spec-042). Backward compatibility maintained for reading.
Changed YI format to date-prefixed YYYYMMDD-xxxxxxxx (spec-041). Clean rewrite - legacy format not supported.
Added YI (run_id) for rollback lineage (spec-036). Made YQ, YX required. YT now optional. Removed YM (use YX.d instead).

YV.description: Schema version number (integer). Used for migration compatibility. Increments by 1 for each major schema change (no decimal versions).
YV.note: Version is always an integer. Versions may skip numbers for architectural changes (e.g., v10→v12). No decimal versions.

YG.description: Difficulty level with optional sub-level. Format: 'level' or 'level:sublevel'. Level must be a valid slug from config/puzzle-levels.json. Sub-level (1-3) provides finer granularity within each level.
YG.note: Policy: enrich_if_absent — source YG preserved if valid; classifier runs only when missing. See config/sgf-property-policies.json.

YT.description: Comma-separated technique tags (alphabetically sorted, deduplicated). Each tag must exist in config/tags.json. Optional if tags cannot be determined.
YT.note: Policy: enrich_if_absent — source YT preserved if present; tags computed only when missing. See config/sgf-property-policies.json.

YH.description: Compact hints format: pipe-separated list of 1-3 hints. Format: hint1|hint2|hint3.
examples: Focus on corner|snapback|The throw-in at ba creates a dead shape
YH.note: YH1/YH2/YH3 are REMOVED in v8 - no backward compatibility.

YR.description: Refutation move coordinates (comma-separated). Common wrong first moves for educational feedback.

YC.description: Board region identifier. Enables region-based filtering.

YQ.description: Quality metrics: measures DATA RICHNESS (how well documented). Quality level (q:1-5) indicates documentation completeness, refutation count (rc) is number of wrong-move branches, comment level (hc: 0=none, 1=correctness markers, 2=teaching text), analysis completeness (ac: 0=untouched, 1=enriched, 2=ai_solved, 3=verified).

rc.description: Refutation count - number of wrong-move branches in solution tree

hc.description: Comment level - 0 (no comments), 1 (correctness markers only), 2 (genuine teaching text)

YX.description: Complexity metrics: measures PUZZLE DIFFICULTY (how hard to solve). All metrics computable from tree analysis - NO AI required.

d.description: Solution depth - moves in main correct line

r.description: Reading count - total nodes in solution tree (workload)

s.description: Stone count - total stones on board (position complexity)

u.description: Uniqueness - 1 if single correct first move, 0 if miai (multiple correct)

a.description: Average refutation depth - mean depth of wrong-move branches (0 if no refutations)

YK.description: Ko context for puzzles involving ko fights.

YO.description: Move order flexibility. Default (no YO property) means strict order required.

YL.description: Collection membership: comma-separated entries referencing config/collections.json. Each entry is either a bare slug, slug:CHAPTER/POSITION (chapter string + 1-based integer position), or slug:POSITION (position-only, no chapter). The forward-slash separates chapter from position unambiguously.
YL.note: Each slug must exist in config/collections.json. Bare slugs are valid (backward compatible). When :CHAPTER/POSITION suffix is present, publish stage groups by chapter then sorts by position within chapter, instead of alphabetical hash ordering. Chapter is a string identifier (supports numeric or named chapters like 'intro-a'). Position-only format (:N) omits chapter. Set by source tools (e.g., weiqi101) or analyze stage.

YM.description: Pipeline metadata JSON: embeds trace_id directly in SGF for cross-stage correlation. Format: {"t":"<trace_id>","i":"<run_id>"}. Set at ingest, read by analyze/publish. Replaces trace map sidecar files.
YM.note: v13: YM now contains all pipeline metadata. Fields: 't' (trace_id), 'i' (run_id — was YI). 'f' (original_filename) is set at ingest but stripped at publish — publish log records it independently. 's' (source adapter ID — was YS) removed in v13. All fields optional except 't'. Kept in published files for forensic debugging via trace_id.

YM_v5.reason: Redundant with YX.d (solution_depth). Use YX.d instead. Note: YM was re-introduced in v12 with a completely different meaning (pipeline metadata JSON).

YS.reason: Folded into YM.s field. Source adapter ID now lives inside YM JSON. Parser still reads legacy YS for backward compatibility.

YI.reason: Folded into YM.i field. Run ID now lives inside YM JSON. Parser still reads legacy YI for backward compatibility.

YH1.reason: Replaced by YH compact format. No backward compatibility.

YH2.reason: Replaced by YH compact format. No backward compatibility.

YH3.reason: Replaced by YH compact format. No backward compatibility.

standard_properties.description: Standard SGF FF[4] properties that YenGo uses

GN.description: Game/puzzle name. Format: YENGO-{16-char-hex}. Must match filename (minus .sgf).

SO.description: Source. MUST be empty or absent. Provenance stored in pipeline state, not SGF.

C.description: Comment. Root-level C[] is REMOVED by enricher. Move variation C[] (pedagogical feedback) is PRESERVED.

comment_handling.description: Rules for C[] comment property handling. See config/sgf-property-policies.json for configurable policy.

root_level_comment.reason: Root-level comments are preserved by default (configurable via preserve_root_comment). HTML/CJK stripped.

move_variation_comment.reason: Move variation comments explain WHY moves are correct/wrong. Essential for learning. Examples: 'Correct!', 'Wrong - white lives'.

rules: No empty lines in output SGF
Compact single-line format preferred for small puzzles
Properties in logical order: standard SGF props → YenGo props → solution tree

move_annotation_properties.description: Standard FF[4] move annotations to use in solution trees

TE.description: Tesuji marker. Use on correct moves.

BM.description: Bad move marker. Use on refutation/wrong moves.

enricher_responsibilities.description: Property handling is config-driven. See config/sgf-property-policies.json for the declarative policy registry.
notes: Property policies are declarative — add/modify entries in sgf-property-policies.json without code changes
BLOCKED: dropped at parse time (DT, CA, RE, AP, etc.)
PRESERVE: source value kept as-is (AN, PL, GC, PB, PW)
HARDCODE: forced to fixed value (FF=4, GM=1)
OVERRIDE: always overwritten (GN by publisher, YV to current version)
ENRICH_IF_ABSENT: source value preserved; pipeline computes only when missing (YG, YT, YH, YC, YK, YO, YR, YL, SZ)
ENRICH_IF_PARTIAL: re-computed if value fails validation (YQ, YX)
REMOVE: parsed but deleted before output (SO)
CONFIGURABLE: behavior controlled by runtime flag (C[] via preserve_root_comment)
SZ defaults to 19 when absent (enrich_if_absent with default_value)

validation_rules: YV must be present in root node (current version: 12)
YG must reference valid level slug from config/puzzle-levels.json
All tags in YT must exist in config/tags.json
YT tags must be comma-separated, alphabetically sorted, deduplicated
YL slugs must exist in config/collections.json and be comma-separated/sorted
Only one YG property allowed per SGF
Only one YT property allowed per SGF
YH uses pipe-separated format: hint1|hint2|hint3 (max 3 hints)
YH1/YH2/YH3 are NOT supported (removed in v8)
SO must be empty or absent
Move variation C[] must be preserved
No empty lines in SGF output

example_sgf.note: v12 adds YM pipeline metadata JSON. No YH1/YH2/YH3. No root C[]. No SO[]. GN is YENGO-{hash}.
